title: Suspect to appear in court Monday and ‘many’ charges expected, Pirro says
Live updates: Officials identify suspect in White House correspondents’ dinner shooting
description: The suspect will be charged with three counts and will be arraigned Monday, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.

title: A Washington Post reporter describes hearing possible shots
Trump says he sees shootings as a reflection of his impact
Inside the chaotic correspondents’ dinner shooting: ‘Get down, get down!’
description: For the third time in three years, President Donald Trump found himself under the threat of gunfire. This time the Washington press corps was in the room.

title: Trump says he sees shootings as a reflection of his impact
description: “I hate to say I’m honored,” the president says of his repeated brushes with violence, including two assassination attempts during his 2024 campaign.

Cabinet secretaries rush from ballroom; Secret Service seen jumping on Trump
A suspect is in custody after Trump is rushed from correspondents' dinner
description: President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.

title: Top buyers of Trump meme coin join Mike Tyson, others at lavish Mar-a-Lago event
description: Top holders of Donald Trump’s meme coin got a conference at Mar-a-Lago, featuring Trump-branded swag and potential facetime, even as the coin has lost value.

title: Trump ousts National Science Board members
description: Members of the independent board that guides the National Science Foundation said they received a notice from the White House that their position was being terminated.

title: The US pulls out of peace talks with Iran
description: President Trump says he has cancelled the trip of his representatives to the Iran peace talks in Islamabad, shortly after Iran's Foreign Minister left Pakistan.

title: Trump’s changes to Reflecting Pool worry preservationists, locals
description: Some warned that the president’s blue-tinted resurfacing would distort the experience for visitors and make the national symbol resemble a “large lap pool.”

title: Dirk Kempthorne, former Idaho governor and U.S. Interior secretary, dies at 74
description: Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.

title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
description: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.

title: Iran says no direct talks planned as U.S. envoys set to leave for Pakistan
description: Iran’s foreign minister arrived in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The White House said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would leave for Islamabad on Saturday.

title: Democrats could actually retake the Senate. Here are the races to watch.
description: The map still favors Republicans, but Trump’s declining popularity — particularly on the economy — makes Democrats’ once long-shot bid to retake the Senate more feasible than ever before.

title: Trump eager to show off latest White House project to King Charles
description: The president’s black granite path is the newest change to the West Wing, following gold lettering, Oval Office gilding and a “Presidential Walk of Fame.”

title: Trump’s political headwinds pile up after a week of setbacks
description: New polls showed voters prefer Democrats on the economy for the first time since 2010 and Republicans disapprove of Trump’s handling of inflation.

title: DOJ’s big payouts to Trump allies could undermine past investigations
description: Since Trump’s return to the White House last year, the Justice Department has paid more than $8.5 million to resolve legal claims brought by allies and supporters.

title: Week in Politics: Congress and the Iran war; Trump's approval ratings
description: We look at what Congress might do as the 60-day window to vote on the war per the War Powers Act draws near, as well as what the latest polls say about President Trump's approval ratings.

title: Trump to attend White House Correspondents' Association dinner. What's it like?
description: President Trump is attending Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner where presidents have historically poked fun at themselves while celebrating the journalists who cover them, something Trump has not easily done in the past.

title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
description: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

title: The rising cost of fertilizer and fuel prices is pushing some farmers to the brink
description: In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.

title: Trump and Congress cut funding for Planned Parenthood. Can Botox keep it afloat?
description: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

title: Trump administration moves to add firing squad to federal execution methods
description: The Justice Department also said it was reviving a lethal injection protocol used during President Donald Trump’s first term.

title: Trump orders more access to psychedelics like LSD, psilocybin and ibogaine
description: The president is directing federal agencies to boost research into the drugs and support clinical trials, moves championed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

title: White House loosens rules for preserving presidential records
description: After the Justice Department said a post-Watergate records law is unconstitutional, White House lawyers established a new policy that experts say weakens safeguards.

title: DoJ drops Powell probe, paving the way to confirm Kevin Warsh
description: The Justice Department is dropping its investigation of Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell, paving the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to lead the Fed.
